description: The chart only shows data from 2015 to 2021, a period where Belgium's renewable electricity share fluctuates between roughly 16% and 26% with notable dips (e.g., dropping from ~20% in 2015 to ~16% in 2016, and from ~26% in 2020 to ~22% in 2021), suggesting stagnation or instability. However, the title still claims to cover 1990–2021, hiding the dramatic long-term growth story from near 0% in the 1990s to over 20% — making Belgium's impressive renewable energy expansion appear much less significant than it actually was.
